It’s much faster, 100% accurate and always true to size.

Do you plan to fix this? Otherwise we’ll have to find another tool instead of XD than Figma.

Figma to App

# From MVPs to native apps. Pixel-perfect, every time.

Bravo doesn't approximate your design — it interprets it. Your Figma file is the spec, the build, and the deploy artifact. Native iOS and Android, every detail intact.

The Signal Path

## Three steps. One file in, app out.

Bravo's workflow is the simplest path from design to App Store. No code generation. No translation. The file is the app.

01

### Design in Figma

Bravo doesn't have templates. You build the file. We won't dilute your design — every screen ships exactly how you drew it.

02

### Tag your layers

A Bravo Tag is a tiny string in a layer name. The Bravo Tag plugin adds them in bulk and validates the file is ready to ship.

Bravo To Go is our managed build service. Our team turns your Figma file into a shipping app, end to end.

results.title: How to Design and Build a Real Working Mobile App in 48 hrs ...
results.content:
## Final Result

I did run into a few snags trying to publish after I made some manual adjustments but after some troubleshooting and guidance from Buzzy’s support team, I successfully published a fully functional app. The app allowed users to create and manage budget categories in real-time, directly within Figma. I realized that when publishing issues arise, clearing the workspace and republishing from Figma is a good fix.

🗃️

Blog / / Case Study

# How to Design and Build a Real Working Mobile App in 48 hrs. (Using Figma AI + No Code)

  

If you think it’s cool to tell a prompt to design something, imagine being able to tell that prompt to research, design and code it for you. I’m going to show you the entire behind-the-scenes process of how I challenged myself to use Figma’s new AI features to do user research and analysis, generate my UI designs, prototype them, and then turn it all into a real app --and I did it all in just 48 hours. Here's what happened...

## The Problem [...] 🗃️

Want the Figma Project Files? Download them here →

In just 48 hours, I built a complete working app—something that would have taken months without the magic of AI! If you enjoyed this, be sure to check out Buzzy and have a look at my course where I've taught thousands of students the foundations, tools and techniques to become skillful and mindful product designers.

results.title: Instagram
results.content:
Figma had to shut off their new Make Design AI tool yesterday with some drama.  
  
Here's what happened...  
  
Someone was prompting for a weather app. After repeatedly asking for new generations, all of the designs look VERY similar to Apple's Weather app. This is leading people to think that Figma's AI was actually trained on real apps.  
  
TL;DR, it wasn't. Because it's not even generating designs! Figma's AI is assembling designs from it's massive design system.  
  
It seems like either the design system is not big enough, or the components tagged to be used in weather apps is not large-scale enough, or (most likely) the prompts that assemble the components into a workable design are being handicapped a lot.
